---
title: "prompt redesign"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


01. Rewrite the function generating the assumption plots to the agreed design. Guarantee that the regerssion branch has the same look and feel (drop plotl.lm()), where necessary.

2. In the vignette: replace the whole decision logic from levene.test median to leven.test mean. give short sentence why. 
2b  you must change the internal functon levene.test(). Gauge your result against the existin levene.test() in the car package. change function documentation and examples 

2c: for p-value calculations in the assumption diagnostics  use always raw residuasl 

In the code make the distinction obvious by 

raw_residuals <- residuals(fit)
std_residuals <- rstandard(fit)

Make one sentence in vignette to justify  this design choice 

4. change the design of the diagnostic panel to the latest agreed version in you MWE 
Justify in vignette the different design choices for the display of the residuals: 
especially route1 : z-residuals route3: internally studentized residuals for grpahical displya 
   
5. change the wording of the current    general linear model diagnostic section to reflect the current design. 

6. check for consistency throughout after the changes 



8, Change the formula in appendix to mean based Levene test 



1

11. vingnette add, where it fits best:  Panels 1--3 display internally studentised residuals for visual comparison.
In Route 1, the spread panel displays absolute raw residuals scaled by the
residual standard error. The formal normality and variance p-values in the
outer title are computed from raw model residuals
Last: check the whole vignette for consistency. Are all changes everywhere applied 
homoscedasticity is assessed by testing whether absolute internally
studentised model residuals differ across groups.


Route 1 diagnostic panels display pooled z-scaled model residuals,
\(e_i/\hat{\sigma}\).
And:
Formal normality and variance p-values are computed on raw model residuals;
because Shapiro--Wilk, Anderson--Darling, Levene, and Bartlett are invariant
to common scaling, this is equivalent to using \(e_i/\hat{\sigma}\).


add something like
### Residual scale used for tests and plots

Route 1: raw residuals for tests; e/sigma(fit) for plots
Route 3: raw residuals for SW/AD/BP p-values; rstandard() for plots


#use diamanods for all enas, also in the box pltos of fihser, welch and t-test variants 


Route 1 diagnostic panel: 1×3
Route 3 diagnostic panel: 2×2

content as last mwe.
